STANDARDS REVIEW of peek-overlay.ts changes.

The file /Users/shivam94/.pi/agent/extensions/orchestrator/peek-overlay.ts had replay mode code removed. The file is untracked in git (no diff available). Review the CURRENT state of the file.

Changes made (5 edits):
1. Removed `let _replayMode: boolean = false;` state variable
2. Removed `setReplayMode()` export function + JSDoc
3. Removed `_replayMode = false;` from `clearViewerState()`
4. Replaced ternary `const bottomText = _replayMode ? "─ Esc: close " : "─ Esc: close  xx′: abort ";` with constant `const bottomText = "─ Esc: close  xx′: abort ";`
5. Removed `if (_replayMode) return;` guard + comment in `handleInput()`

Standards sources in this repo:
- tsconfig.json (extends parent tsconfig, includes all .ts)
- AGENTS.md in orchestrator extension dir
- No eslint, prettier, CONTRIBUTING.md, or CODING_STANDARDS.md

Smell baseline (always apply, judgement calls only, repo overrides):
- Mysterious Name — function/variable/type name doesn't reveal what it does
- Duplicated Code — same logic shape in more than one hunk
- Feature Envy — method reaches into another object's data more than its own
- Data Clumps — same fields/params travel together
- Primitive Obsession — primitive standing in for domain concept
- Repeated Switches — same switch/if-cascade on same type recurs
- Shotgun Surgery — one logical change forces scattered edits
- Divergent Change — one file edited for several unrelated reasons
- Speculative Generality — abstraction/params/hooks for needs spec doesn't have
- Message Chains — long a.b().c().d() navigation
- Middle Man — class/function mostly just delegates onward
- Refused Bequest — subclass ignores/overrides most of what it inherits

Read the full current peek-overlay.ts file. Report — per file/hunk where relevant — (a) every place the diff violates a documented standard: cite the standard (file + the rule); and (b) any baseline smell you spot: name it and quote the hunk. Distinguish hard violations from judgement calls. Skip anything tooling enforces. Under 400 words.
